name: Taj Mahal
overview: The Taj Mahal is one of the most famous monuments in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
history: Construction began in 1632 and took over 20 years to complete. Around 20,000 craftsmen worked on it.
architecture: Built from white marble with intricate carvings, calligraphy, and precious stones.
facts: The Taj Mahal changes color depending on sunlight.
It is considered one of the Seven Wonders of the World.

name: Colosseum
overview: The Colosseum is the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built.
history: Construction began under Emperor Vespasian and was completed by his son Titus.
architecture: Could hold up to 50,000 spectators with a complex system of underground tunnels.
facts: It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.

name: Eiffel Tower
overview: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower and the most famous landmark in Paris.
history: Built for the 1889 World's Fair and initially criticized before becoming a global symbol of France.
architecture: Stands 330 meters tall and was the tallest man-made structure for 41 years.

name: Great Wall
overview: Massive series of fortifications built across northern China to defend against invasions.
history: Construction started more than 2,000 years ago and continued through multiple dynasties.
architecture: Stretches over 21,000 kilometers with watchtowers, barracks, and fortresses.
facts: Visible from space in low orbit.

name: Big Ben
overview: The nickname for the Great Bell of the clock at the Palace of Westminster.
history: Completed in 1859 and became a symbol of British democracy.
architecture: Stands 96 meters tall with Gothic Revival architecture.
facts: One of the most accurate large clocks.
Survived bombing in World War II.

name: Acropolis
overview: Ancient citadel with temples, most notably the Parthenon.
history: Built during the reign of Pericles in Classical Greece.
architecture: Masterpiece of Classical Greek architecture featuring the iconic Parthenon.
facts: One of the most important surviving buildings of Classical Greece.

name: Angkor
overview: The largest religious monument in the world, Angkor Wat is a masterpiece.
history: Built in the early 12th century as a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu.
architecture: Surrounded by a massive moat and features intricate stone carvings.
facts: Largest religious monument in the world.
Symbol of Cambodia appearing on its flag.

name: Liberty Island
history: A gift from France to the United States, designed by Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi.
architecture: 151 feet tall with iron framework designed by Gustave Eiffel.
facts: One of the most recognizable statues in the world.

name: Mont Saint-Michel
overview: Medieval abbey on a rocky tidal island, iconic French landmark.
history: Founded in the 8th century, it became a pilgrimage destination.
architecture: Romanesque and Gothic architecture perched on a rocky island.
facts: One of Europe's most recognizable landmarks.
